Meta Data v9s

Event Name/Title

Conquering the enemy of lust

Event Date

29 Dec 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Lakshmi Anand

Completed

24 Nov 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-087-Conquering%20The%20Enemy%20Of%20Lust%20(Mumbai)-SB_7_13_26-dt_89_12_29.mp3

NA

Verse

NA

Tags – Places

Click here to enter text.

Tags – Pastimes

Prahalad Maharaj and Avadhut brahmin, Yamunacharya prayers, Avadhut Brahmin and 24 gurus

Tags – Persons

Prahalad Maharaj, Avadhut Brahmin, Srila Prabhuupada, Krishna, Maya, Yamunacharya

Tags – Themes

Detachment, Humility, Keep Krishna in the center, Service for Krishna

Lecture Trancription:

tatrāpi dam-patīnāṁ ca
sukhāyānyāpanuttaye
karmāṇi kurvatāṁ dṛṣṭvā
nivṛtto ’smi viparyayam

[SB 7.13.26]

[In this human form of life, men and women unite for the sensual pleasure of sex, but by actual experience we have observed that none of them are happy. Therefore, seeing the contrary results, I have stopped taking part in materialistic activities.]

Purport

As stated by Prahlāda Mahārāja, yan maithunādi-gṛhamedhi-sukhaṁ hi tuccham. Man and woman both seek sexual enjoyment, and when they are united by the ritualistic ceremony of marriage, they are happy for some time, but finally there is dissension, and thus there are so many cases of separation and divorce. Although every man and woman is actually eager to enjoy life through sexual unity, the result is disunity and distress. Marriage is recommended to give men and women a concession for restricted sex life, which is also recommended in Bhagavad-gītā by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi: sex life not against the principles of religion is Kṛṣṇa. Every living entity is always eager to enjoy sex life because materialistic life consists of eating, sleeping, sex and fear. In animal life, eating, sleeping, sexual enjoyment and fear cannot be regulated, but for human society the plan is that although men, like animals, must be allowed to eat, sleep, enjoy sex and take protection from fear, they must be regulated. The Vedic plan for eating recommends that one take yajṣa-śiṣṭa, or prasāda, food offered to Kṛṣṇa. Yajṣa-śiṣṭāśinaḥ santo mucyante sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ: “The devotees of the Lord are released from all kinds of sins because they eat food that is offered first for sacrifice.” (Bg. 3.13) In material life, one commits sinful activities, especially in eating, and because of sinful activities one is condemned by nature’s laws to accept another body, which is imposed as punishment. Sex and eating are essential, and therefore they are offered to human society under Vedic restrictions so that according to the Vedic injunctions people may eat, sleep, enjoy sex, be protected from fearful life and gradually be elevated and liberated from the punishment of material existence. Thus the Vedic injunctions for marriage offer a concession to human society, the idea being that a man and woman united in a ritualistic marriage ceremony should help one another advance in spiritual life. Unfortunately, especially in this age, men and women unite for unrestricted sexual enjoyment. Thus they are victimized, being obliged to take rebirth in the forms of animals to fulfill their animalistic propensities. The Vedic injunctions therefore warn, nāyaṁ deho deha-bhājāṁ nṛloke kaṣṭān kāmān arhate viḍ-bhujāṁ ye. One should not enjoy sex life like hogs, and eat everything, even to the limit of stool. A human being should eat prasāda offered to the Deity and should enjoy sex life according to the Vedic injunctions. He should engage himself in the business of Kṛṣṇa consciousness, he should save himself from the fearful condition of material existence, and he should sleep only to recover from fatigue due to working hard. The learned brāhmaṇa said that since everything is misused by fruitive workers, he had retired from all fruitive activities.

[In this human form of life, a man and woman mule for the sensual pleasure of sex. But by actual experience we have observed (hat none of them are happy. Therefore, seeing the contrary results, I have stopped taking part in the materialistic activities."]

An intelligent person sees through ears

Hmm... This great brahmin, this self-realized personality, who is here instructing Prahlada Maharaj is explaining by his own personal experience and realization, why he has taken to the renounced order of life. One who observes the, spiritual nature with the spirit of detachment, see everything as it is. The ways of this material world are very obvious. The lessons to be learnt are quite clear. But if we look upon this world with attachment, then we cannot see anything in truth. We see everything only according to our relative spirit of enjoyment and therefore the truth that Krishna is trying to reveal to every element of existence, is clouded. We cannot perceive anything as it is, but a person who is detached from the material enjoyment, wherever he goes, whatever he sees, whatever he hears, whatever he experiences, is simply echoing the words of his spiritual master and the words of the scriptures. Therefore, it is said that an intelligent man sees through his ears, does not see through his eyes. He hears from the shastras, he hears from the guru and sadhus, exactly what is the real nature of this material existence and because he is detached from the spirit of enjoyment, he can see the truth. He can see the message of God everywhere and what is the message of God speaking through this material existence? This great Avadhuta brahmin is describing, due to the temporary nature of everything within this world, ultimately, it is the place of suffering, it is a place of misery.

Sexual Pleasure -Envy towards Supreme Lord

The ultimate pleasure of this world is the enjoyment of sex that is shared between the male and the female.

puṁsaḥ striyā mithunī-bhāvam etaṁ
tayor mitho hṛdaya-granthim āhuḥ
ato gṛha-kṣetra-sutāpta-vittair
janasya moho ’yam ahaṁ mameti

[SB 5.5.8]

[The attraction between male and female is the basic principle of material existence. On the basis of this misconception, which ties together the hearts of the male and female, one becomes attracted to his body, home, property, children, relatives and wealth. In this way one increases life’s illusions and thinks in terms of “I and mine.”]

That the basic principle for the entire material existence is the attraction between the male and the female. This attraction brings the male and female together to unite for sexual enjoyment. The product of such sexual enjoyment is the endlessly increasing attachment to children, to home, to relatives, to land, to prestige and so on and so on and so on. In this way one cannot see anything in truth because one sees everything colored by the image of "I and mine". One sees everything in terms of how I am this body and everything that is mine is for my enjoyment and whatever is not mine we have endless long desires to possess for our sense gratification. So, in this way the sex pleasure which is the ultimate experience of gratification in this material existence, is the basic principle of this entire material world. It is due to this spell of sex desire that the entire creation continues. It is such a powerful urge that is impregnated within the hearts of all the conditioned souls, indeed, according to the acharyas, it is the ultimate expression of envy towards the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Because Krishna is the enjoyer of everything and to the degree, we want to enjoy his property, to that degree we are expressing our envy towards him and everything is his property. Therefore, to the degree we intensely want to enjoy, to that degree we are envious of God. It is said in the shastras this material world is mithuna agara that it is the place where literally every living entity in this material encagement is shackled by the chains of sex desire.

Grihastha Ashram-Keep Krishna in center

Therefore, Vedic civilization, in fact actually every religious civilization, tries to differentiate man from animal by regulating and controlling these sexual propensities. The ideal is always to give it up completely. It is never an easy thing. But if one is willing and able to make this great sacrifice, it is considered a most worshipable expression of one's devotion to God. Therefore, in the vedic society a sannyasi is always offered respects by all classes of men. Because he is willing to make this sacrifice. A person who is not willing and able to make this great sacrifice at least he should take or she should, take to the grihastha ashram and regulate sexual conduct to at the maximum once per month for the purpose of procreating Krishna conscious children. Otherwise, the man and the woman, they come together only for the service of the Supreme Lord, Sri Krishna. They have no other business together. They raise children in the service of Krishna, they serve the deity in the service of Krishna, they feed the sadhus and the devotees in the service of Krishna. In this way the man and the woman in the grihastha ashram, they make Krishna the center of all of the activities. To the degree we make Krishna the center, to that degree the grihastha is detached from the impulse for sexual enjoyment and to the degree they do not put Krishna in the center, to that very degree they will be vulnerable to wasting their precious life enjoying like the animals.

The greatest illusion of humans

So here the Avadhuta, because he was detached, he was simply witnessing human society, simply watching like an observer and what was his conclusion, his conclusion was, he was watching how so many people, practically everybody, they are happily, so-called happily enjoying sex life, the unity of man and woman and he is seeing how practically in every instance, this so-called most unifying of all principles, creates the greatest disunity in one's own heart. Why? Because it is just one of the greatest illusions in all the existence. People become mad after this pleasure. But Prahlada Maharaj explains that this pleasure is nothing more than just simply rubbing your hands together (maharaj rubs hand[maharaj:] sound by rubbing the hands together). From a factual point of view, from an unbiased point of view, simply rubbing your hands together gives you a little pleasure. There is no more significance in this ultimate satisfaction of the world than this. Prahlad Maharaj explains, it is just simply scratching an itch. The more you scratch an itch, the more aggravates it and the more it itches. If you want the itch to go away, you tolerate it and let it go through its own course. But if you scratch it, it simply increases the pain, the suffering and the discomfort. So, although the Maya, the illusory energy promises us so much happiness, prosperity and pleasure through the unity of man and woman through sex life; we should know that when these promises come into our hearts, it is simply a great illusion. It is Maya's supreme weapon to cast us into a condition of bondage and that is all. Sense gratification, Krishna says, has a beginning and an end and therefore wise men do not delight in it.

ye hi saṁsparśa-jā bhogā
duḥkha-yonaya eva te
ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya
na teṣu ramate budhaḥ

[Bg 5.22]

[An intelligent person does not take part in the sources of misery, which are due to contact with the material senses. O son of Kuntī, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, and so the wise man does not delight in them.]

The fire of lust

It lasts for a few seconds and then it is gone and we are more disturbed than we were in the first place for more. That is the condition. Krishna says in the Bhagavad gita, that lust is like a fire, it is insatiable, it is constantly burning and the more you feed it, the more hunger the fire of lust, becomes within your mind. When you feed lusty propensities with sense gratification, what you are doing, is taking your worst enemy and you are feeding it sumptuously and you are arming him with all of your weapons and all of your strength. Sense gratification is factually a suicide, towards one's spiritual life! Krishna says in Gita,

śrī-bhagavān uvāca
kāma eṣa krodha eṣa
rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā
viddhy enam iha vairiṇam

[Bg 3.27]

[The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: It is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.]

That it is lust only, which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world. So, know that this desire to enjoy sense gratification, which is held in its supreme manifestation to the sex impulse, is the greatest most sinful enemy in our life. To one, who is hoping to advance in the spiritual life, there is no greater and sinful enemy than this. Therefore, an intelligent person, Krishna says, does not in anyway, befriend his enemy. He conquers his enemy. This is the difference between the intelligent person and a fool. An intelligent person fights his enemy, conquers his enemy. Krishna says in the very beginning, “Conquer the sinful enemy of lust". By keeping one's senses constantly engaged in the devotional service of the lord by tolerating. But a foolish person is very anxious to befriend his enemy. We feed our enemy very nicely, and provide him with all the weapons to destroy us. That is the process of sense gratification. Therefore, this great Avadhuta brahmin, he is seeing how the whole material society is under the spell, the mystical spell of sexual attraction and how due to this there is the repetition of birth and death continuously going on. So much frustration, so much bondage, so much disunity. Therefore, simply on the basis of this and this alone, the Brahman is explaining, he has completely renounced this world. He says here, in this human form of life man and woman unite for sensual pleasure for sex but by actual experience we have observed that none of them are happy. Therefore, seeing the contrary results, I have stopped taking part in the materialistic activities. Srila Prabhupada describes, "The more one indulges in sex life, the more one becomes entangled in the endless complexities of material existence.

Detachment leads to simple and happy life

The more one able to be detached, the more one can live a simple, humble and happy life in this world. "

Krishna says in Gita,

tāni sarvāṇi saṁyamya
yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi
tasya prajṣā pratiṣṭhitā

[Bg 2.61]

[One who restrains his senses, keeping them under full control, and fixes his consciousness upon Me, is known as a man of steady intelligence.]

That the process of fixing your consciousness on Krishna is that you must restrain your senses from the objects. You cannot he Krishna conscious if you are allowing your senses to be engaged in sense pleasures and simultaneously it is impossible to fix, to restrain yourselves unless you fix your consciousness upon Krishna." Therefore, the process of overcoming this demoniac all-devouring enemy of lust that, somehow or the other we have welcomed to come and live within our minds, is to constantly the urges of minds and senses and in the process of tolerating the urges of mind and senses, we must keep them constantly engaged in the service of Lord Hari. It is said, " The ideal mind is the devil's workshop". If our mind is not filled with Krishna, it will be filled with Maya. There is no in between. There is either Krishna or Maya. We are the marginal energy of the lord. That means we are standing between the external inferior energy and the superior spiritual energy. But please know that you are never in between. Sometimes we think that the marginal energy is in between, but there is no in-between. You are either in Maya or you are in Krishna Consciousness.

Maya’s service

If you are sleeping in Maya, Krishna consciousness means to be awake. Krishna consciousness means to be conscious. You cannot be conscious of Krishna when you are sleeping. Prabhupada says," Sleeping means laying in the lap of Maya". Therefore, we reduce our sleep as far as possible. In fact, on one occasion, Prabhupada saw so many devotees sleeping during his classes, that he said that as far as he was concerned, oversleeping was worse than illicit sex! Emm? Because this unconscious...remaining too prolonged in the state of unconsciousness influences so much the mode of ignorance that the mode of passion is following right behind! So, therefore it is very important to know that the marginal energy of the lord, the jivatma, he always has the independence to either be under Krishna's control or Maya's control, but he is never in between. Just like in the material world, there is the law of gravity where something is either going up or something is going down. It is not that you throw something in the air and it just stays in one place. It is either going up or going down. Now it is easier by the laws of nature to let a thing go down. Correct. Because it is the very nature of this place where everything is coming down. This material world everything is under the laws of gravity. What is the law of gravity'? It brings everything down. So, in this material world everyone is under the laws of gravity and in this spiritual life also we are under the laws of gravity. As soon as we are the slightest of bit inattentive or lazy, the gravitational pull of Maya pulls us down towards sense gratification, down towards bondage. It is a great struggle and it takes a great determination to continuously go up, or in the river the same principle is there. If you are swimming very hard, you can swim upstream. But as soon as you stop you never stay one place. You are immediately pulled down ­streams towards the ocean of birth and death. Or when you are pedaling a bicycle up a hill, it is a very hard struggle. But if you stop for a second, you don't stay at one place. You go down! So, understand that working against the whole current of material existence while trying to be Krishna conscious; everything in this material world is trying to pull our mind and senses down to sense enjoyment. This whole material existence is trying to pull us towards ultimately sex pleasure. Therefore, a devotee knows that an ideal mind is a devil's workshop. If we are not struggling and striving to keep our mind and senses engaged in Krishna's service, then as soon as we stop, we are engaged in Maya's service.

Engage mind and senses in the service of Lord

The precious energy of our human life is going to offer oblations of our valuable human energy into the all-consuming, all-devouring fire of our sinful enemy in the form of lust. But a devotee of the lord, he utilizes his precious energy to offer oblations for the pleasure of the Supreme Personality of Godhead in the form of devotional service. Therefore, there are nine processes of devotional service and one must be very careful to always be keeping his mind and senses engaged in one of these nine processes. The Padma-Purana describes,

sarvopādhi-vinirmuktaṁ
tat-paratvena nirmalam
hṛṣīkeṇa hṛṣīkeśa-
sevanaṁ bhaktir ucyate

[CC Madhya 19.170]

[“ ‘Bhakti, or devotional service, means engaging all our senses in the service of the Lord, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the master of all the senses. When the spirit soul renders service unto the Supreme, there are two side effects. One is freed from all material designations, and one’s senses are purified simply by being employed in the service of the Lord.’]

That, " By engaging the mind and the senses in the service of the lord of the senses, one becomes purified. Through this process of purification paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate [Bg 2.59] one develops a higher taste. One's natural inclination to love Krishna is awakened and then in that state of always being engaged in Krishna's service, then the service of the senses becomes a disgusting consideration. As Srila Yamunacharya has prayed, that one time, he describes, he was a king and he was very fond of sexual life. But he says that," Now that I have tasted the ultimate sweetness of the ultimate service of Lord Hari through the process of bhakti, even the ultimate pleasure of this material world, sex life, is so unpalatable and disgusting to me, that the thought of it causes me to curl my lips in distaste and spit at the thought of it. By hearing and chanting the glories of the lord in the association of the devotees and by engaging in the service of the lord, by worshipping the deities, by chanting the holy name, by remembering Krishna, by sitting and attentively hearing Srimad Bhagavatam, by offering prayers to the lord, by rendering service as a personal servitor to the lord and his devotees, by carrying out the orders of the spiritual master, by befriending the lord, and by surrendering everything to the lord; these are the nine processes by which twenty-four hours a day, Krishna is giving us the opportunity to purify our lives by being engaged in his service. Whenever the invitation of Maya comes into our lives, we should be very careful. We should not. It is said that if you play with the fire then you are burnt. Lust is compared to fire. If you play with fire, you will get burnt. Therefore, we should he always engaged in playing with Krishna by serving his devotees.

Goswami and Godasa

Humility, the ultimate expression of humility comes when we control our senses, when we are willing to say "No" to Maya and be the servant of Krishna's servants. Prabhupada says that the difference between Gosvami and a Godasa, a servant of the senses and a master of the senses; is not that the impulses are not there. But the power to say "No"': The desire, the determination to say, "No"! When the tongue wants to taste something which is not according to the God's desire, we have to be willing to say. "No"! When the ears want to hear something that is not conducive to Krishna consciousness, we must be willing and determined to say, "No"' When the eyes want to see something that is not conducive for our Krishna consciousness, we must be willing to say, "No"! Then we are Gosvami. Godasa is one simply agrees with all the proposals that the Maya gives from within and without. So, therefore we must become very tolerant and we must become very enthusiastic at all times to work very hard. Prabhupada ends his purport practically by explaining that sleep is only to recover from the fatigue due to working hard. So, devotees are meant to work very hard. Devotees must never he lazy. All the great acharyas condemn laziness. When we are lazy, we are just opening the doors for Maya to rush into our mind and take up the control. Therefore, we must be very active, we must be very busy in hearing the glories of the lord, in chanting the glories of the lord and in rendering service by executing the instruction of the higher authorities. In this way if we are working very hard, then we sleep simply to recover from the fatigue and we get up and we work hard. This is the process of Krishna consciousness. This is the process by which we can be immuned from the influences which are constantly trying to drag us down towards sense gratification. The more we control our senses, the more we follow these principles; the more we conceive the truth being revealed by the lord in every situation of this material life. Why is it that the grihasthas, when they have marital problems, they are generally always more inclined towards revealing their problems and taking shelter from a sannyasi or a brahmachari than another grihastha? From the material point of view, it does not make sense! Because, " What in the world does a sannyasi know about marriage problems'.' Often times he is never been married! He never had relationship with a woman. He never had children! What does he know about these problems?" Hmm? He never has any like his own experience. But factually, simply because he is detached, by watching others he can see everything clear, through and through of what is taking place! One who is, it is just like, if you are in the middle of the battlefield, all you know is who is attacking right immediately. But you don’t know what else is going on because you are just too involved in your own little battle. But if you are above in the helicopter, then you can see everything as it is! Because you are detached from the whole situation! So similarly, one who is detached, just by seeing the activities of the grihasthas, a person can understand the true nature of a family life far better than somebody who is immersed and entered in all the problems and complexities. Hmm? This is exactly what this Avadhuta brahmin is trying to say. That the more you are detached, the more can you understand the truth and the facts of the situation and the more you are attached, the more you cannot even see what is taking place in your life!

How to use everything in the service of Krishna

There is a story of another in the eleventh Canto of Bhagavatam about one Avadhuta who has approached and asked, "Who is your spiritual master?" and he said, how many? Twenty-four spiritual masters. He says, "Because I am completely detached from enjoying or getting entangled in any of these activities of this material world, I have learned everything about the material life simply by watching, simply by observing. By observing the moon, by observing the ocean, by observing the sun, by observing the pigeons, by observing the bees, by observing a prostitute of a name Pingala and in so many ways he was watching and observing and because of detachment, so many truths were revealed to him, of the true nature of this material existence and the true nature of the saintly person. This is the great opulence of detachment. Through detachment we can see the things as they are. According to Rupa Gosvami what is the ultimate detachment

prapaṣcikatayā buddhyā
hari-sambandhī-vastunaḥ
mumukṣubhiḥ parityago
phalgu vairāgyaṁ kathyate

[NOD 1.2.256]

To use everything in the service of Krishna, that is the detachment. Nothing for our own sense enjoyment, everything, our mind, our senses, our wealth, our ability, everything. If it is fully utilized to our maximum capacity in the service of Krishna - that is the ultimate detachment, through this process we can see everything as it is. How do you use everything in the service of Krishna? By hearing from the great souls, by associating with the great souls and by executing their instructions as our life and soul and not deviating. That is the process of self-realization.

Thank You very much.

Is there any questions?

Q/A Transcription

Devotee: Krishna is love of God. Is there any parallel of the love of God?

Maharaj: Parallel as love of God? Krishnadas Kaviraj Gosvami explains that love, prema is like gold and lust is that same propensity, when it is misdirected away from Krishna it becomes iron. Both gold and iron are metals. But one is of great value and one is quite worse. When propensity for affection is directed towards Krishna, that is love.
When it is directed away from Krsna to the objects of sense gratification, that is lust. So, it is the same - the love and lust are the same energy. But

Devotee: But what is the sex life?

Maharaj: The basic principle of lust is sex life. But that sex life manifests itself in so many varieties of ways in this world. Subtle sex life is profit adoration and distinction. Gross sex life is using our gross senses to enjoy the objects, the various objects of material life. Lust is the desire to enjoy anything, anyone, anywhere in this material world. Love is to always have Krishna in the center of our life. Hmm? Yes?

Devotee: (not audible 40:06-40:27)

Maharaj: Whenever you are quoting Prabhupada you don’t quote exactly. You shouldn’t’ say Prabhupada said this, you should say Prabhupada said something like this. Your way of quoting Prabhupada is little bit off. Prabhupada does not say exactly like that. Prabhupada says that, "This is a very dangerous process that, if you give your senses so much sense gratification then ultimately you become so fatigued and tired that you become detached. But this process is never recommended by the acharyas and sadhus. The process recommended is detachment in tolerance. Because do you know how much ghee you have to put on the fire to put the fire off? Especially when the fire is huge and hungry within your heart of lust, you know how much ghee it takes and if it is not quite enough what happens, it increases the fire by millions of time. So, it is never recommended, although it is possible, it is so dangerous, it is never recommended. But if someone is absolutely against controlling his senses, then you should burn yourself out. If you put thousand billions of ghee on the fire, it is dangerous.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
